MINI MINUTES:

James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party James Peterson Jr.

RULING: DEFENDANT LEHIGH SOUTHWEST CEMENT COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ITS EXPERT DESIGNATION PURSUANT TO 2034.720

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company's Motion for an Order Granting Leave to Amend its Expert Designation Pursuant to CCP 2034.720 is denied. Defendant failed to submit a declaration showing that Shannon H. Gaffney was omitted from its expert designation as a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. (CCP 2034.720 (c)(1).) Defendant also failed to file Exhibits A-C to the declaration of Daniel Trowbridge in support of its motion and failed to provide the Court with courtesy copies of Exhibits A-C pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(b). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The Court considered all papers filed the parties including the surreplies by filed Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc., Defendant Pierce Lathing Co., Defendant Flour Corporation, and Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company. The trial date is May 28, 2019 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is June 7, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 23, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 10, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 24, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 8, 2019. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
